rationale: An LLM at this capability ceiling (basic HTTP scripting, trivial resource exhaustion exploits) transfers modestly to execution tasks — primarily through generating functional PowerShell/LOLBin command sequences and recalling documented techniques — but cannot assist with the adaptive, behavioral-evasion-aware tradecraft that modern EDR at MIL2/MIL3 requires. The estimated uplift is modest (~8 percentage points over the 30% baseline), reflecting that LLM assistance accelerates technique recall and scripting but cannot overcome real-time EDR behavioral analysis or help the actor adapt when initial execution attempts are caught. The distribution is relatively narrow because execution at this stage has a well-understood set of bottlenecks (EDR behavioral detection) that remain largely invariant to the type of LLM assistance this capability ceiling can provide.
